name: Wheel Plow
json: Once this game, when you use the "Farmland" or Cultivation" action space with the first person you place in a round, you can plow 2 additional fields.
comp: Once this game, when you use the "Farmland" or "Cultivation" action space with the first person you place in a round, you can plow 2 additional fields.
note: JSON drops the opening quotation mark before Cultivation ('or Cultivation"'); compendium prints 'or "Cultivation"'. Punctuation-only difference.

name: Manger
json: During scoring, if your pastures cover at least 6/7/8/10 farm yard spaces, you get 1/2/3/4 bonus points.
comp: During scoring, if your pastures cover at least 6/7/8/10 farmyard spaces, you get 1/2/3/4 bonus points.
note: JSON splits 'farmyard' into two words 'farm yard'; compendium prints the correct single word 'farmyard'. Name (Manger) agrees.

name: Oriental Fireplace
json: At any time: Vegetable → 4 Food; Sheep → 3 Food; Cattle → 5 Food "Bake Bread" action: Grain → 2 Food
comp: At any time, you may convert goods to food as follows: Vegetable ➝ 4 food; Sheep ➝ 3 food; Cattle ➝ 5 food. Whenever you bake bread, you may convert: Grain ➝ 2 food.
note: Same conversions/rates/timing; JSON is a terse table, compendium spells the same rules out in full sentences (footnote "15 Ponton on BGG 9" removed).

name: Silage
json: In each returning home phase after which there is no harvest, you can pay exactly 1 grain—even from a field-to breed exactly one type of animal.
comp: In each returning home phase after which there is no harvest, you can pay exactly 1 grain - even from a field - to breed exactly one type of animal.
note: Same words; JSON uses em-dash 'grain—even' and glued 'field-to' vs compendium spaced hyphens 'grain - even from a field - to breed' — punctuation/spacing only.

name: Stable Planner
json: Add 3, 6, and 9 to the current round. You can place 1 stable on each corresponding round space. A the start of these rounds (not earlier), you can build the stable at no cost.
comp: Add 3, 6, and 9 to the current round. You can place 1 stable on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds (not earlier), you can build the stable at no cost.
note: JSON has typo "A the start" (missing t); compendium reads "At the start". Otherwise identical.

name: Fellow Grazer
json: During scoring, you get 2 bonus points of reach pasture you have covering at least 3 farmyard spaces.
comp: During scoring, you get 2 bonus points for each pasture you have covering at least 3 farmyard spaces.
note: JSON has typo "points of reach pasture"; compendium reads correctly "points for each pasture".

name: Seasonal Worker
json: Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you get 1 additional grain. From round 6 on, you can choose to get 1 vegetable instead.
comp: Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you get 1 additional grain. From Round 6 on, you can choose to get 1 vegetable instead.
note: Capitalization only: compendium prints "From Round 6 on" vs JSON "From round 6 on"; all other text and the name ("Seasonal Worker") match.

name: Wood Carrier
json: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood fore each improvement in front of you.
comp: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood for each improvement in front of you.
note: JSON has typo "wood fore each"; compendium printed text correctly reads "wood for each".

name: Firewood Collector
json: Each time you use the "Farmland", "Grain Seeds", Grain Utilization", or "Cultivation" action space, at the end of that turn, you get 1 wood.
comp: Each time you use the "Farmland", "Grain Seeds", "Grain Utilization", or "Cultivation" action space, at the end of that turn, you get 1 wood.
note: JSON omits the opening quotation mark before Grain Utilization; compendium quotes it properly. Punctuation-only.

name: Drudgery Reeve
json: If there are still 1/3/6/9 complete rounds left to play, you immediately get 1/2/3/4 wood. During scoring, each player with 1+/2+/3+ building resources of each type gets 1/3/5 bonus points.
comp: If there are still 3/6/9 complete rounds left to play, you immediately get 2/3/4 wood. During scoring, each player with 1+/2+/3+ building resources of each type gets 1/3/5 bonus points.
note: JSON has four wood tiers (1/3/6/9 rounds -> 1/2/3/4 wood) vs printed card's three (3/6/9 -> 2/3/4 wood); JSON's extra low tier appears copied from the adjacent Animal Reeve card. Scoring clause identical.

name: Harpooner
json: Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space you can also pay 1 wood to get 1 food for each person you have, and 1 reed
comp: Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space you can also pay 1 wood to get 1 food for each person you have, and 1 reed.
note: JSON omits the trailing sentence-ending period that the compendium prints after "1 reed"; otherwise identical.

name: Conjurer
json: Each time you use the "traveling Players" accumulation space, you get an additional 1 wood and 1 grain.
comp: Each time you use the "Traveling Players" accumulation space, you get an additional 1 wood and 1 grain.
note: Capitalization only: JSON lowercases the action-space name as "traveling Players"; compendium prints "Traveling Players".

name: Moonshine
json: Randomly select an occupation in your hand. Either play it for an occupation cost of 2 food, or give it to the player to your left.
comp: Determine a random occupation in your hand. Immediately play it for an occupation cost of 2 food or give it to the player to your left.
note: Same meaning, reworded: JSON "Randomly select...Either play it" vs comp "Determine a random...Immediately play it"; the added "Immediately" is emphasis on already-implied timing, no mechanical change.

name: Store of Experience
json: If you have 0-4/5/6/7 occupations left in hand, you immediately get 1 stone/reed/clay/wood.
comp: If you have 0-3/4/5/6-7 occupations left in hand, you immediately get 1 stone/reed/clay/wood.
note: Compendium thresholds are 0-3/4/5/6-7 (both raw_after and normalized text agree) vs JSON 0-4/5/6/7 — different occupation-count buckets changing which resource is gained.

name: Market Stall
json: You immediately get 1 vegetable. (Effectively, you are exchanging 1 grain for 1 vegetable).
comp: You immediately get 1 vegetable. (Effectively, you are exchanging 1 grain for 1 vegetable.)
note: Period placement inside vs outside closing paren: compendium 'vegetable.)' vs JSON 'vegetable).'; otherwise identical.

name: Feedyard
json: This card can hold 1 animal for each pasture you have, even different types. After the breeding phase of each harvest, you get 1 food for each unused spot on this card.
comp: This card can hold 1 animal for each pasture you have, even different types. After the breeding phase of each harvest, you receive 1 food for each unused spot on this card.
note: JSON "you get 1 food" vs compendium "you receive 1 food"; same meaning, different verb.

name: Stockyard
json: This card can hold up to 3 animals of the same type. (It is not considered a pasture).
comp: This card can hold up to 3 animals of the same type. (It is not considered a pasture.)
note: Period placement: compendium 'pasture.)' (inside paren) vs JSON 'pasture).' (outside paren); only punctuation differs.

name: Hawktower
json: Place a stone room on round space 12. If you live in a stone house at the start of the round, you can build the stone room at no cost. Otherwise, discard the stone room.
comp: Place a stone room on round space 12. If you live in a stone house at the start of that round, you can build the stone room at no cost. Otherwise, discard the stone room.
note: Compendium reads "at the start of that round"; JSON reads "at the start of the round" — same meaning, different wording.

name: Grassland Harrow
json: Add 1 to the current round for each building resource in your supply and place 1 field on the corresponding round space. A the start of the round, you can plow the field.
comp: Add 1 to the current round for each building resource in your supply and place 1 field on the corresponding round space. At the start of that round, you can plow the field.
note: JSON has typo "A the start" (missing 't'); compendium reads "At the start of that round" (also "that round" vs JSON "the round").

name: Agrarian Fences
json: Each time you use the "Grain Utilization" action space, you can take a "Build Fences" action instead of one of the two actions provide by the action space.
comp: Each time you use the "Grain Utilization" action space, you can take a "Build Fences" action instead of one of the two actions provided by the action space.
note: JSON has typo "actions provide by" (missing 'd'); compendium correctly reads "actions provided by".

name: Toolbox
json: In the work phase, after each turn in which you build at least 1 room, stable, or fence, you can build the "Joinery", "pottery", or Basketmaker's Workshop" major improvement.
comp: In the work phase, after each turn in which you build at least 1 room, stable or fence, you can build the "Joinery", "Pottery", or "Basketmaker's Workshop" major improvement.
note: Only punctuation/capitalization differs: JSON has Oxford comma 'stable, or', lowercase '"pottery"', and a missing opening quote before Basketmaker's; compendium has 'stable or', '"Pottery"', '"Basketmaker's Workshop"'. Same words, no meaning change.

name: Forestry Studies
json: Each time after you use the "Forest" accumulation space, you can return 2 wood to that space to play 1 occupation without paying an occupation costs.
comp: Each time after you use the "Forest" accumulation space, you can return 2 wood to that space to play 1 occupation without paying an occupation cost.
note: JSON has number-agreement typo "an occupation costs"; compendium prints the correct singular "an occupation cost".

name: Cookery Lesson
json: Each time you use a "Lesson" action space and a cooking improvement on the same turn, you get 1 bonus point.
comp: Each time you use a "Lessons" action space and a Cooking improvement on the same turn, you get 1 bonus point.
note: Compendium quotes the "Lessons" action space; JSON drops the s ("Lesson"), a typo (secondary: compendium "Cooking" vs JSON lowercase "cooking").

name: Wood Palisades
json: Instead of a fence piece, you can place 2 wood from your supply on the fence spaces at the edge of your farmyard. These fence spaces with 2 wood are worth 1 bonus point.
comp: Instead of a fence piece, you can place 2 wood on fence spaces at the edge of your farmyard. These fence spaces with 2 wood are worth 1 bonus point.
note: JSON adds "from your supply" and the article "the" before "fence spaces"; game meaning is identical (wood is placed from supply regardless).

name: Pottery Yard
json: During the scoring, if there are at least 2 orthogonally adjacent unused spaces in your farm, you get 2 bonus points. (You still get the negative points for those unused spaces.
comp: During scoring, if there at least 2 orthogonally adjacent unused spaces in your farmyard, you get 2 bonus points. (You still get the negative points for those unused spaces.)
note: Compendium drops the verb "are" ("if there at least" vs JSON's correct "if there are at least"); also JSON omits the closing ")", adds "the" (During the scoring), and uses "farm" vs compendium "farmyard" — no meaning change; the dropped word makes it a typo.

name: Kettle
json: At any time, you can exchange 1/3/5 grain for 3/4/5 food and 0/1/2 bonus points.
comp: At any time, you can exchange 1/3/5 grain for 3/4/5 food plus 0/1/2 bonus points.
note: Compendium prints "food plus 0/1/2" where JSON has "food and 0/1/2"; same game meaning, different connective word.

name: Special Food
json: The next time you take animals from an accumulation space and accommodate all of them on your farm, you get 1 bonus point for each of these animals.
comp: Once this game, the next time you take animals from an accumulation space and accommodate all of them on your farm, you get 1 bonus point for each such animal.
note: Compendium opens "Once this game," (redundant with "the next time," which already fires exactly once) and reads "each such animal"; JSON omits the opener and says "each of these animals" — same game meaning, different phrasing.

name: Hook Knife
json: Once this game, when you have 9/8/7/6/5/5 sheep on your farm in a 1-/2-3-/4-/5-/6- player game, you immediately get 2 bonus points.
comp: Once this game, when you have 9/8/7/6/5/5 sheep on your farm in a 1-/2-/3-/4-/5-/6-player game, you immediately get 2 bonus points.
note: Real difference but purely punctuation/spacing: JSON drops the slash in "2-/3-" (renders "2-3-") and adds a space in "6- player" vs compendium "6-player"; no letter/word or game-meaning change, so text-punct not text-typo.

name: Future Building Site
json: Up until all other farmyard spaces are used, you cannot use the unused spaces that are orthogonally adjacent to your house.
comp: Until all other farmyard spaces are used, you cannot use spaces that are orthogonally adjacent to your house.
note: Compendium "Until ... spaces"; JSON "Up until ... the unused spaces" — extra "unused" qualifier is mechanically redundant, so same game meaning, different wording.

name: Chophouse
json: Each time you use the "Grain/Vegetable Seed" action space, place 1 food on each of the next 3/2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
comp: Each time you use the "Grain Seeds"/"Vegetable Seeds" action space, place 1 food on each of the next 3/2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
note: Compendium spells out the two action-space names in full ("Grain Seeds"/"Vegetable Seeds") while JSON compresses to "Grain/Vegetable Seed"; same spaces, same game meaning.

name: Scales
json: Each time after you place an improvement or occupation in front of you, if you then have the same number of improvements and occupations in play, you get 2 food.
comp: Each time after you place an improvement or occupation in front of you, if you have the same number of improvements and occupations in play, you get 2 food.
note: JSON adds "then" ("if you then have"); compendium prints "if you have" — same game meaning, wording only.

name: Sculpture Course
json: At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you can use this card to exchange your choice of 1 wood for 2 food, or 1 stone for 4 food.
comp: At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you can use this card to exchange either 1 wood for 2 food, or 1 stone for 4 food.
note: Compendium says "exchange either" where JSON says "exchange your choice of"; same game meaning, different phrasing.

name: Tumbrel
json: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 food. Each time after you take an unconditional "Sow" action, you get 1 food for each stable you have.
comp: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 food. Each time after you take a "Sow" action, you get 1 food for each stable you have.
note: JSON adds the qualifier "unconditional" before "Sow" action, restricting which Sow actions trigger the food; the printed compendium card has no such qualifier.

name: Maintenance Premium
json: Place 3 food on this card. Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you get 1 food from this card. Each time you renovate restock this card to 3 food.
comp: Place 3 food on this card. Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you get 1 food from this card. Each time you renovate, restock this card to 3 food.
note: Compendium has a comma after "renovate" ("Each time you renovate, restock"); JSON omits it ("renovate restock"). Punctuation only; identical wording/mechanics.

name: Food Chest
json: If you play this card on the "Major Improvement" action space, you immediately get 4 food. Otherwise, you get only 2 food.
comp: If you play this card on the "Major Improvement" action space, you immediately get 4 food. If you play it on another action space, you immediately get 2 food.
note: Compendium spells out "If you play it on another action space, you immediately get 2 food"; JSON collapses this to "Otherwise, you get only 2 food" — same game meaning (any non-Major-Improvement space = 2 food), different phrasing.

name: Tasting
json: Each time you use a "Lessons" action space, before paying the occupation cost, you can exchange 1 grain for 4 food.
comp: Each time you use a "Lessons" action space, before you pay the occupation cost, you can exchange 1 grain for 4 food.
note: Compendium "before you pay the occupation cost" vs JSON "before paying the occupation cost"; identical game meaning, phrasing only.

name: Potters Market
json: At any time, you can pay 4 clay and 2 food. If you do, place 1 vegetable on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the vegetable.
comp: At any time, you can pay 3 clay and 2 food. If you do, place 1 vegetable on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the vegetable.
note: Cost differs: compendium prints "3 clay and 2 food", JSON says "4 clay and 2 food" — a real mechanical cost change.

name: New Purchase
json: Before the start of each round that ends with a harvest, you can buy one of each of the following crops: 2 Food → 1 Grain; 4 Food → 1 Vegetable
comp: Before the start of each round that ends with a harvest, you can buy one of each of the following crops: 2 food ➝ 1 grain; 4 food ➝ 1 vegetable.
note: Formatting only (capitalization / arrow glyph / spacing).

name: Harvest House
json: When you play this card, if the number of completed harvests is equal to the number of occupations you played, you immediately get 1 food, 1 grain, and 1 vegetable.
comp: When you play this card, if the number of completed harvests is equal to the number of occupations you played, you immediately get 1 food, 1 grain and 1 vegetable.
note: Oxford comma only: JSON '1 grain, and 1 vegetable' vs compendium '1 grain and 1 vegetable'; text otherwise identical.

name: Gift Basket
json: When you play this card, if you have exactly 2/3/4/5 rooms, you immediately get 1 vegetable/food/grain/vegetable.
comp: When you play this card, if you have exactly 2/3/4/5 rooms, you immediately get 1 vegetable/food/grain/ vegetable.
note: Formatting only (capitalization / arrow glyph / spacing).

name: Reed Belt
json: Place 1 reed on each of the remaining space for rounds 5, 8, 10, and 12. At the start of these rounds, you get the reed.
comp: Place 1 reed on each remaining space for rounds 5, 8, 10, and 12. At the start of these rounds, you get the reed.
note: JSON has "on each of the remaining space", compendium "on each remaining space"; extra "of the" is a wording difference, same game meaning.

name: Handcart
json: Before each work phase, you can take 1 building resource from at most one wood/clay/reed/stone accumulation space containing at least 6/5/4/4 building resources of the same type.
comp: Before the start of each work phase, you can take 1 building resource from a wood/clay/reed/stone accumulation space with at least 6/5/4/4 building resources of the same type. You can only take 1 resource per round.
note: Same game meaning (1 resource per work phase from one qualifying space, same 6/5/4/4 thresholds), phrased differently: JSON 'from at most one ... space' vs compendium 'from a ... space. You can only take 1 resource per round.'; also 'Before each' vs 'Before the start of each'.

name: Value Assets
json: After each harvest, you can buy exactly one of the following goods: 1 Food → 1 Wood; 1 Food → 1 Clay; 2 Food → 1 Reed; 2 Food → 1 Stone
comp: After each harvest, you can buy exactly one of the following building resources: 1 food ➝ 1 wood; 1 food ➝ 1 clay; 2 food ➝ 1 reed; 2 food ➝ 1 stone.
note: JSON descriptor "the following goods" vs compendium "the following building resources"; identical exchange list, so same meaning phrased differently (casing/arrow ignored).

name: Acorns Basket
json: Place 1 wild boar on each of the 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the wild boar.
comp: Place 1 wild boar on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the wild boar.
note: Compendium says "each of the next 2 round spaces"; JSON omits "next" — clarifying word, same game meaning.

name: Little Stick Knitter
json: From Round 5 on, each time you use the "sheep Market" accumulation space, you can also take a "Family Growth with Room Only" action.
comp: From Round 5 on, each time you use the "Sheep Market" accumulation space, you can also take a "Family Growth with Room Only" action.
note: Compendium capitalizes the proper name "Sheep Market"; JSON writes it lowercase as "sheep Market". Only capitalization differs.

name: Confidant
json: Place 1 food from your supply on each of the next 2, 3, or 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food back and your choice of a "sow" or "Build Fences" action.
comp: Place 1 food from your supply on each of the next 2, 3, or 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food back and your choice of a "Sow" or "Build Fences" action.
note: Compendium capitalizes the action name "Sow"; JSON has lowercase "sow" — capitalization-only difference, same meaning.

name: Master Bricklayer
json: Each time you build a major improvement, reduce the stone cost by the number of rooms you have built onto you initial house.
comp: Each time you build a major improvement, reduce the stone cost by the number of rooms you have built onto your initial house.
note: JSON has typo "onto you initial house" (missing r); compendium reads "onto your initial house".

name: Organic Farmer
json: During the scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each pasture containing at least 1 animal while having unused capacity for at least three more animals.
comp: During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each pasture containing at least 1 animal while having unused capacity for at least three more animals.
note: Compendium reads "During scoring"; JSON adds "the" ("During the scoring") — same game meaning, wording only.

name: Clutterer
json: During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each card played after this on that has "accumulation space(s)" in its text.
comp: During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each card played after this one that has "accumulation space(s)" in its text.
note: JSON has typo "after this on that" (missing "e" in "one"); compendium reads "after this one that".

name: Case Builder
json: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 good of each of the following types, If you have at least 2 of that good in your supply already, food, grain, vegetable, reed, wood.
comp: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 good of each of the following types, if you have at least 2 of that good already: food, grain, vegetable, reed, wood.
note: JSON adds "in your supply", capitalizes "If", and uses a comma before the list where compendium uses a colon; same game meaning.

name: Moral Crusader
json: Immediately before the start of each round, if there are goods on the remaining round space that are promised to you, you get 1 food.
comp: Immediately before the start of each round, if there are goods on remaining round spaces that are promised to you, you get 1 food.
note: Compendium "remaining round spaces" (plural, no "the") vs JSON "the remaining round space" (singular, with "the"); same game meaning, differently phrased.

name: Rustic
json: For each clay room you build, you get 2 food and 1 bonus point. (this does not apply to stone rooms and renovated wood rooms.)
comp: For each clay room you build, you get 2 food and 1 bonus point. (This does not apply to stone rooms and renovated wood rooms).
note: Compendium capitalizes "(This" and places the period outside the paren ("rooms)."); JSON lowercases "(this" and puts the period inside ("rooms.)"). Only capitalization and punctuation differ.

name: Childless
json: At the start of each round, if you have at least 3 rooms but only 2 people, you get 1 food and 1 crop of your choice (grain or vegetable)
comp: At the start of each round, if you have at least 3 rooms but only 2 people, you get 1 food and 1 crop of your choice (grain or vegetable.)
note: Compendium closes the parenthetical with a period inside "(grain or vegetable.)"; JSON has no period. Punctuation only; identical wording and game meaning.

name: Shoreforester
json: When you play this card and each time 1 reed is placed on an empty "Reed Bank" accumulation space in the preparation phase, you get 1 wood.
comp: When you play this card, and each time 1 reed is placed on an empty "Reed Bank" accumulation space in the preparation phase, you get 1 wood.
note: Compendium has a comma after "this card," ("...this card, and each time..."); JSON omits it. Punctuation-only, same meaning.

name: Informant
json: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood. After each work phase, if you have more stone than clay in your supply, you get 1 wood.
comp: After each work phase, if you have more stone than clay in your supply, you get 1 wood.
note: Compendium Informant (B117) has only the "after each work phase" effect; the leading "When you play this card... you get 1 wood" in raw_before belongs to the prior card (Shoreforester B116). JSON adds an on-play +1 wood the compendium omits.

name: Lumberjack
json: You immediately get 1 wood. Additionally, place 1 wood on each of the next round spaces, up to the number of fences you built. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood.
comp: Immediately place 1 wood on each of the next round spaces, up to the number of fences you built. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood.
note: JSON adds an extra immediate 1-wood gain ("You immediately get 1 wood. Additionally,") not present in the compendium, which reads "Immediately place 1 wood on each of the next round spaces...".

name: Trimmer
json: In each work phase, after you enclose at least one farmyard space, you get 2 stone. (Subdividing an existing pasture does not count.)
comp: Each time after you enclose at least one farmyard space, you get 2 stone. (Subdividing an existing pasture does not count.)
note: Compendium "Each time after you enclose" fires per enclosure event; JSON "In each work phase" caps the reward to once per work phase — differing trigger frequency.

name: Seducer
json: When you play this card in round 5 or later, you can immediately pay 1 stone, 1 grain, 1 vegetable, and 1 sheep to take a "Family Growth Even without Room" action.
comp: When you play this card in Round 5 or later, you can immediately pay 1 stone, 1 grain, 1 vegetable, and 1 sheep to take a "Family Growth Even without Room" action.
note: Capitalization only: compendium "Round 5" vs JSON "round 5"; rest identical after stripping glued next-card name "Plumber".

name: Plumber
json: Each time after you use the "Major Improvement" action space, you can take a "renovation" action, paying 2 clay or 2 stone less for the renovation.
comp: Each time after you use the "Major Improvement" action space, you can take a "Renovation" action for 2 clay or 2 stone less.
note: Same meaning: compendium terse "for 2 clay or 2 stone less" vs JSON spelled-out "paying 2 clay or 2 stone less for the renovation" (plus "Renovation" vs "renovation" casing).

name: Full Peasant
json: Each time after you use the "Grain Utilization" or "Fencing" action space while the other is unoccupied, you can pay 1 food to use the other space with the same person.
comp: Each time after you use the "Grain Utilization" or "Fencing" action space while the other is unoccupied, you can pay 1 food to use that other space with the same person.
note: Compendium reads "use that other space"; JSON reads "use the other space" — one-word difference, identical game meaning.

name: Forest Scientist
json: In the returning home phase of each round, if there is no wood left on the game board, you get 1 food—from round 5 on, even 2 food.
comp: In the returning home phase of each round, if there is no wood left on the game board, you get 1 food - from round 5 on, even 2 food.
note: Only the dash differs: JSON em-dash without spaces ('food—from') vs compendium spaced hyphen ('food - from'); all words identical.

name: Clay Warden
json: Each time another player uses the "Hollow" accumulation space, you get 1 clay. In a 3-/4-player game, you also get 1 additional clay/food.
comp: Each time another player uses the "Hollow" accumulation space, you get 1 clay. In a 3-/4-player game, you also get 1 clay/food.
note: JSON adds clarifying word "additional" ("1 additional clay/food" vs compendium "1 clay/food"); identical game meaning.

name: Collier
json: Each time after you use the "Clay Pit" or "Hollow" accumulation space, you get 1 wood. On "Clay Pit" you also get 1 additional reed.
comp: Each time after you use the "Clay Pit" accumulation space, you get 1 reed and 1 wood. With 3 or more players, you get 1 additional wood on the "Hollow" accumulation space.
note: Compendium gives reed+wood only on Clay Pit and the Hollow wood bonus is 3+ player-only (confirmed by the "3+ player card" note); JSON grants 1 wood on Clay Pit OR Hollow in all games, so the 2-player Hollow benefit differs.

name: Open Air Farmer
json: When you play this card, you remove exactly 3 stables in your supply from play to build a pasture covering 2 farmyard spaces. You only need to pay a total of 2 wood for fences
comp: When you play this card, if you have at least 3 stables in your supply, remove 3 stables in your supply from play and build a pasture covering 2 farmyard spaces. You only need to pay a total of 2 wood for the fences.
note: Same game meaning phrased differently: compendium spells out precondition "if you have at least 3 stables... remove 3... and build" while JSON uses "remove exactly 3 stables... to build"; JSON also drops "the" before "fences". No mechanical change.

name: Little Peasant
json: You immediately get 1 stone. As long as you live in a wooden house with exactly 2 rooms, actions spaces—excluding Meeting Place—are not considered occupied for you.
comp: You immediately get 1 stone. As long as you live in a wooden house with exactly 2 rooms, action spaces—excluding Meeting Place—are not considered occupied for you.
note: JSON typo "actions spaces" for compendium's correct "action spaces".

name: Housemaster
json: During scoring, total the point values of your major improvements. The smallest value counts double. If the total is at least 5/7/9/11, you get 1/2/3/4 bonus points.
comp: During scoring, total the values of your major improvements. The smallest value counts double. If the total is at least 5/7/9/11, you get 1/2/3/4 bonus points.
note: JSON "total the point values" vs compendium "total the values"; same meaning (major improvement values are point values).

name: Sheep Keeper
json: You can only play this card if you have less than 7 sheep. Once this game, when you have 7 sheep on your farm, you immediately get 3 bonus points and 2 food.
comp: You can only play this card if you have less than 7 sheep. Once this game, when you have 7 sheep in your farmyard, you immediately get 3 bonus points and 2 food.
note: Compendium prints "in your farmyard"; JSON prints "on your farm" — same game meaning, different phrasing.

name: Art Teacher
json: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood and 1 reed. Each time you pay an occupation cost, you can use food from the "traveling Players" accumulation space.
comp: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood and 1 reed. Each time you pay an occupation cost, you can use food from the "Traveling Players" accumulation space.
note: Capitalization only: compendium '"Traveling Players"' vs JSON '"traveling Players"'; text otherwise identical.

name: Storehouse Keeper
json: Each time you use the "resource Market" action space, you also get your choice of 1 clay or 1 grain.
comp: Each time you use the "Resource Market" action space, you also get your choice of 1 clay or 1 grain.
note: Capitalization only: compendium prints "Resource Market" (capital R, matching the action-space name), JSON has lowercase "resource Market".

name: Salter
json: At any time, you can pay 1 sheep/wild boar/cattle from you farm. If you do, place 1 food on each of the next 3/5/7 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
comp: At any time, you can pay 1 sheep/wild boar/cattle from your farmyard. If you do, place 1 food on each of the next 3/5/7 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
note: JSON has ungrammatical typo "from you farm" (missing 'r' in "your"); compendium prints "from your farmyard".

name: Weakling
json: Each time it is your turn in the work phase, if there are one or more accumulation spaces with 5+ goods on them and you do not use any of them, you get 1 vegetable.
comp: Each time it is your turn in the work phase, if there are 1 or more accumulation spaces with 5+ goods on them and you do not use any of them, you get 1 vegetable.
note: Compendium prints numeral "1 or more"; JSON spells out "one or more" — same meaning, wording difference.

name: Game Provider
json: Immediately before each harvest, you can discard 1/3/4 grain from different fields to get 1/2/3 wild boars.
comp: Immediately before each harvest, you can discard 1/3/4 grain from different fields to receive 1/2/3 wild boars.
note: Compendium prints "to receive" where JSON has "to get"; identical game meaning, purely a wording variant.

name: Pasture Master
json: Each time you renovate, you get 2 food and 1 additional animal of the respective type in each of your pastures with stable.
comp: Each time you renovate, you get 1 additional animal of the respective type in each of your pastures with stable.
note: JSON adds "2 food and" which the printed compendium text does not grant; compendium only gives 1 additional animal per pasture-with-stable on renovate.

name: Feed Fence
json: For each new stable you build, you get 1 food-for your last one, get 3 food. Each time you build stables, you can build exactly 1 stable for 1 clay instead of wood.
comp: For each new stable you build, you get 1 food - for your last one, get 3 food. Each time you build stables, you can build exactly 1 stable for 1 clay instead of wood.
note: Formatting only (capitalization / arrow glyph / spacing).

name: Small Potter's Oven
json: When you play this card, you immediately get 5 food. Each time before you get a "Bake Bread" action, you can build the "Clay Oven" or "stone Oven" major improvement.
comp: When you play this card, you immediately get 5 food. Each time before you get a "Bake Bread" action, you can build the "Clay Oven" or "Stone Oven" major improvement.
note: JSON lowercases the proper name as "stone Oven"; compendium prints "Stone Oven" — capitalization only.

name: Slurry
json: Slurry Spreader
comp: Slurry
note: Compendium prints the card name as just "Slurry" (the word immediately before the C071 id; text matches), whereas JSON names it "Slurry Spreader".

name: Green Grocer
json: At the start of each round, you can make exactly one of the following exchanges: 1 Cattle → 1 Vegetable; 1 Vegetable → 1 Cattle; 2 Sheep → 1 Vegetable; 1 Vegetable → 2 Sheep; 2 Food → 1 Grain; 1 Grain → 2 Food
comp: At the start of each round, you can make exactly one of the following exchanges: 1 Cattle → 1 Vegetable; 1 Vegetable → 1 Cattle; 2 Sheep → 1 Vegetable; 1 Vegetable → 2 Sheep; 2 Food → 1 Grain; 1 Grain → 2 Food.
note: Compendium text ends with a trailing period after "2 Food" (before glued next-card "Collector"); JSON omits it. Punctuation-only difference; footnote noise "51 Uwe via Ponton on BGG 34" stripped from front.

name: Collector
json: This card is an action space for you only. When you use it for the 1st/2nd/3rd/4th time, you get 1 begging marker and 6/7/8/9 different good of your choice.
comp: This card is an action space for you only. When you use it for the 1st/2nd/3rd/4th time, you get 1 begging marker and 6/7/8/9 different goods of your choice.
note: JSON has singular-noun typo "different good"; compendium prints the correct plural "different goods".

name: Bricklayer
json: Each improvement and each renovation cost you 1 clay less. Each room costs you 2 clay less.
comp: Each improvement and each renovation costs you 1 clay less. Each room costs you 2 clay less.
note: Compendium reads "renovation costs you 1 clay less"; JSON drops the 's' ("cost you"), a grammar typo for the singular "each ... and each ..." subject.

name: Soldier
json: During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each stone-wood pair in your supply. You cannot score additional points for the resources scored with this card.
comp: During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each stone-wood pair in your supply. You cannot score additional points for the resources scored with his card.
note: COMPENDIUM typo: prints "resources scored with his card"; JSON correctly reads "this card".

name: Charcoal Burner
json: Each time any player (including you) plays or builds a cooking improvement, you get 1 wood and 1 food.
comp: Each time any player (including you) plays or builds a baking improvement, you get 1 wood and 1 food.
note: Compendium prints "baking improvement"; JSON says "cooking improvement" — distinct improvement categories, so the trigger set differs.

name: Zigzag Harrow
json: You can immediately plow 1 field such that it completes a "zigzag" pattern.
comp: You can immediately plow 1 field such that it competes a "zigzag" pattern.
note: Compendium prints the typo "competes"; JSON has the correct "completes".

name: Game Trade
json: You immediately get 1 wild boar and 1 cattle. (effectively, you are exchanging 2 sheep for 1 wild boar and 1 cattle.)
comp: You immediately get 1 wild boar and 1 cattle. (Effectively, you are exchanging 2 sheep for 1 wild boar and 1 cattle.)
note: Capitalization only: compendium "(Effectively" vs JSON "(effectively"; verified in normalized PDF text.

name: Pioneering Spirit
json: This card is an action space for you only. In rounds 3-5, it provides a "renovation" action. In rounds 6-8, it provides your choice of 1 vegetable, wild boar, or cattle.
comp: This card is an action space for you only. In rounds 3-5, it provides a "Renovation" action. In rounds 6-8, it provides your choice of 1 vegetable, wild boar, or cattle.
note: Capitalization only: compendium capitalizes "Renovation" where JSON uses lowercase "renovation"; all other words identical.

name: Witches' Dance Floor
json: This card is a field that you can sow in, an occupation, and the "Fireplace" major improvement with all of its effects. You can play it only via a "Minor Improvement" action.
comp: This card is a field that you can sow in, an occupation, and the "Fireplace" major improvement with all of its effects. You can play it only via a "minor Improvement" action.
note: Compendium prints lowercase "minor Improvement"; JSON capitalizes "Minor Improvement" — capitalization only, otherwise identical.

name: Storeroom
json: During scoring, you get 1/2 bonus point for each pair of grain plus vegetable you have (considering all crops in your supply and fields), rounded up.
comp: During scoring, you get 1⁄2 bonus point for each pair of grain plus vegetable you have (considering all crops in your supply and fields), rounded up.
note: Formatting only (capitalization / arrow glyph / spacing).

name: Education Bonus
json: After you play your 1st/2nd/3rd/4th/5th/6th occupation this game, you immediately get 1 grain/clay/reed/stone/vegetable/field (not retroactively).
comp: After you play your 1st/2nd/3rd/4th/5th/6th occupation this game, you immediately get 1 grain/clay/reed/stone/ vegetable/field (not retroactively).
note: Formatting only (capitalization / arrow glyph / spacing).

name: Churchyard
json: Place 2 food on each remaining round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the food. (*Occupations and Improvements)
comp: Place 2 food on each remaining round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
note: JSON appends inline clarifying parenthetical '(*Occupations and Improvements)'; compendium prints that clarification as a separate '● Cards must be Occupations and Improvements' bullet (stripped), so its main text omits it. Same game meaning.

name: Earth Oven
json: At any time: Vegetable → 3 Food; Sheep → 2 Food; Wild boar → 3 Food; Cattle → 3 Food "Bake Bread" action: Grain → 2 Food
comp: At any time, you may convert goods to food as follows: Vegetable → 3 Food; Sheep → 2 Food; Wild boar → 3 Food; Cattle → 3 Food. Whenever you bake bread, you may convert: Grain → 2 Food.
note: Identical conversions/values (Veg→3, Sheep→2, Boar→3, Cattle→3, Grain→2 on bake); compendium spells them out in sentences, JSON uses a terse arrow-table.

name: Large Pottery
json: At any time: Clay → 2 Food Scoring: 3/5/6/7 Clay → 1/2/3/4 bonus points
comp: At any time, you can use the Large Pottery to convert 1 Clay to 2 Food. At the end of the game, you may spend 3/5/6/7 Clay from your supply to earn 1/2/3/4 bonus points.
note: Same effect and values (1 Clay→2 Food; 3/5/6/7 Clay→1/2/3/4 pts); JSON is a terse shorthand table, compendium is the spelled-out sentence ("Scoring:"="At the end of the game").

name: Baking Course
json: At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you can take a "Bake Bread" action. "Bake Bread" action: Grain → 2 Food
comp: At the end of each round that does not end with a harvest, you can take a "Bake Bread" action. Whenever you bake bread, you may convert: Grain → 2 Food.
note: Both grant the same end-of-round bake action and the same 1 grain → 2 food baking rate; JSON uses the terse notation '"Bake Bread" action: Grain → 2 Food' while the compendium spells it out as "Whenever you bake bread, you may convert: Grain → 2 Food" — same meaning, different phrasing.

name: Potter Ceramics
json: Each time before you take a "Bake Bread" action, you can exchange 1 clay for 1 grain.
comp: Each time before you take a "Bake Bread" action, you can exchange exactly 1 clay for 1 grain.
note: Compendium prints "exchange exactly 1 clay"; JSON omits the quantity-constraining word "exactly", loosening the fixed one-clay limit.

name: Reader
json: As soon as you have 7 occupations in front of you (including this one), this card provides room for one person.
comp: As soon as you have 6 occupations in front of you (including this one), this card provides room for one person. In the draft variant, you need 7 occupations to play this.
note: Compendium's main effect triggers at 6 occupations (7 only in the draft-variant note it also carries); JSON uses 7 as the main threshold and omits the draft-variant clause entirely.

name: Fish Farmer
json: Each time there is 1/2/3+ food on the "Fishing" accumulation space, you get an additional 2 food on the "Reed Bank"/ "Clay Pit"/ "Forest" accumulation spaces.
comp: Each time there is 1/2/3+ food on the "Fishing" accumulation space, you get an additional 2 food on the "Reed Bank"/ "Clay Pit"/ "Grove" accumulation spaces.
note: Compendium prints "Grove" (an error its own ERRATA bullet flags as needing to be "Forest"); JSON uses the corrected "Forest".

name: Clay Carrier
json: Wne you play this card, you immediately get 2 clay. At any time, but only once per round, you can buy 2 clay for 2 food.
comp: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 clay. At any time, but only once per round, you can buy 2 clay for 2 food.
note: JSON has typo "Wne you play"; compendium reads "When you play"; rest of text identical after stripping the ● clarification bullet and glued next-card name "Renovation Preparer".

name: Hardworking Man
json: This card is an action space for you only. If each other player has more rooms than you, it provides the "Day Laborer", "Building Rooms", and "Major Improvement" actions (all three).
comp: This card is an action space for you only. If each other player has more rooms than you, it provides the "Day Laborer", "Build Rooms", and "Major Improvement" actions (all three).
note: JSON writes the action name as "Building Rooms"; compendium prints "Build Rooms", the correct game action name.

name: Hide Farmer
json: During scoring, you can pay 1 food each for any number of unused farmyard spaces. If you do, you do not lose points for these spaces.
comp: During scoring, you can pay 1 food each for any number of unused farmyard spaces. You do not lose points for these spaces.
note: JSON inserts the connective "If you do," before the second sentence; compendium omits it. Same game meaning.

name: Water Worker
json: Each time after you use the "Fishing" accumulation space or one of the three orthogonally adjacent action spaces, you get 1 additional reed.
comp: Each time after you use the "Fishing" accumulation space or one of the three orthogonally adjacent actions spaces, you get 1 additional reed.
note: Compendium prints the typo "actions spaces"; JSON correctly reads "action spaces". Verified against compendium_normalized.txt (not extraction garble).

name: Porter
json: Each time you take at least 4 of the same building resource from an accumulation space, you get 1additional building resource of the accumulating type and 1 food.
comp: Each time you take at least 4 of the same building resource from an accumulation space, you get 1 additional building resource of the accumulating type and 1 food.
note: JSON runs "1additional" together (dropped space); compendium correctly prints "get 1 additional building resource".

name: Bean Counter
json: Each time you use an action space on round spaces 1 to 8, place 1 food on this card. Each time this cards has 3 food on it, move the food to your supply.
comp: Each time you use an action space on round spaces 1 to 8, place 1 food on this card. Each time this card has 3 food on it, move the food to your supply.
note: JSON has typo "this cards has"; compendium correctly reads "this card has". Rest of text identical.

name: Cabbage Buyer
json: Each time any player (including you) renovates and the builds no/ 1 minor/ 1 major improvement, you can buy 1 vegetable for 3/2/1 food.
comp: Each time any player (including you) renovates and then builds no / 1 minor / 1 major improvement, you can buy 1 vegetable for 3/2/1 food.
note: JSON typo "and the builds" (dropped n); compendium correctly reads "and then builds".

name: Field Doctor
json: Once this game, if you live in a house with exactly 2 rooms surrounded by 4 field tiles, you can use any "Wish for Children" action space even without room.
comp: Once this game, if you live in a house with exactly 2 rooms surrounded by 4 field tiles, you can use any "Family Growth" action space even without room.
note: Printed compendium card names the "Family Growth" action space; JSON uses "Wish for Children" — a different named action space (the compendium's own errata bullet flags this: should be "Wish for Children").
